mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains equity appreciation over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of cases, or interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 60% of low-inflation expansion periods, or Rank IC drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects consistent economic momentum that supports short-term equity price appreciation over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of positive payroll growth months, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 2.0 percent indicates restrictive monetary conditions that compress corporate profit margins and depress equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances when the rate threshold is breached, or the signal loses predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals supply-side driven growth that fails to translate into consumer spending, causing equity underperformance over one month.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a high unemployment regime confirms economic contraction that triggers immediate short-term equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 55% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to generate a negative Rank IC during high unemployment periods.
